successCriteria.description: File `src/modules/ping/ping.model.ts` exists and exports a `PingResponse` interface with a single field `status: string`
File `src/modules/ping/ping.service.interface.ts` exists and exports an `IPingService` interface with a single method `getPing(): PingResponse`
`IPingService` imports `PingResponse` from `./ping.model` (relative import matching the uptime module convention)
Both files follow the exact structural conventions of the existing uptime module: one exported interface per model file, one exported interface per service-interface file, no extra exports or methods

constraints: No dependencies on any other module â€” the ping module is a pure leaf module with zero imports from outside its own directory (except standard library / framework types)

Follow the exact pattern of the existing uptime module: `uptime.model.ts` exports a single interface with one field, `uptime.service.interface.ts` imports the model type and exports a single interface with one method returning that type
Only the two files listed in the phase scope are created: `src/modules/ping/ping.model.ts` and `src/modules/ping/ping.service.interface.ts`
Interface method signatures are contracts â€” `getPing(): PingResponse` is a complete interface method declaration; no method body is expected or required in this phase
outOfScope: Phase 3: Creating Fastify route registration in `src/modules/ping/ping.routes.ts`
Any test files, database schemas, repository implementations, or any other files not explicitly named in the Phase 1 scope

ambiguities.description: The intent says `status: string` but parenthetically notes "(the literal `"ok"`)". This could be interpreted as a TypeScript literal type `status: "ok"` rather than the general `status: string`. The uptime module uses general types (`uptimeSeconds: number`, not a literal), and ARCHITECTURE.md documents `{ status: string }`, so `string` is the canonical choice.
options: Use `status: string` (general type, matching uptime module convention and ARCHITECTURE.md)
Use `status: "ok"` (TypeScript literal type, matching the parenthetical hint)
